mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions, defined by a 0.3 percentage point monthly unemployment rate increase alongside declining nonfarm payrolls, signals an imminent economic contraction that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP combined with rising housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and business confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the combined Rank IC falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing and mining output, which directly boosts corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with SPX returns in more than 60% of rolling 12-month windows, or the forward return spread between high and low INDPRO terciles is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5 percent year-over-year threshold, persistent inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings balances indicate increased household financial buffers and future consumption capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS changes and SPX forward returns remains below 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-savings periods are not significantly higher than low-savings periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market expansion, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns in more than 50% of observations during confirmed expansions, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index below the 100.0 baseline signals weakening manufacturing activity and resolving supply chain bottlenecks into demand shortfalls, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 2-year rolling window.
